specific_location: Wet pasture on north side of US Highway 50, about halfway between Salida and Poncha Springs
description: Bird was clearly an ibis, a long-legged dark-plumaged wader with a downcurved bill, associating with cows and about 300 other ibis in a wet field. Here is a verbatim excerpt from my field notes: "Legs with no trace of red; eye dark, also without trace of red; facial skin dark gray, faintly tinged blue (no red). Red in legs and eyes and facial skin of adjacent WFIBs was evident in direct comparison. Electric blue lines bordering facial skin: one from eye down to base of bill, the other along top of facial skin from eye to eye. Classic GLIB, except bird has a pale bluish orbital ring, and electric blue lines are narrower than I like on a “classic” obvious GLIB."Behaviors: Bird was foraging very actively with many other ibis in a wet pasture, probing into the mud with its bill. The ibis in the pasture were feeding mostly on large earthworms, as far as I could tell, and occasionally smaller prey items.Call: Bird was silent.Plumage: unknown
